items.title: Gap Recruitment Full Stack Website Development
items.teaser: Modern full stack platform with SEO, blog, ATS, and instant content updates. Hardened security, automated CI/CD, and scalable hosting for high traffic.
highlights: Problem: No strong online presence or job workflow.
Process: Next.js SSG plus ISR, Strapi CMS, ATS integration, sitemap automation, Turnstile, CSP, HSTS, CORS.
Outcome: Indexed, fast, secure site with real time publishing and analytics.
items.HeroSummary: Gap Recruitment wanted a secure, scalable, and visually compelling digital presence to reflect its position as a top tier recruitment agency. I engineered a full stack recruitment platform built with Next.js, Strapi, TailwindCSS, and a hardened Node.js backend, featuring a dynamic job board, SEO optimized blog, bot secure contact form, and an automated DevOps pipeline. Beyond functionality, the project prioritized enterprise grade security, search visibility, and infrastructure resilience through advanced server hardening, NGINX configuration, and DevOps automation. The result is a fully managed digital ecosystem that performs, scales, and protects.
items.Problem: Gap Recruitment Services Ltd needed a modern web platform that unified brand presence, content publishing, and recruitment workflows under one secure and scalable system. The main challenges included the absence of a professional SEO ready website, no centralized blog management, lack of job board integration, manual content updates, and exposure to spam and bot attacks via unprotected contact forms. They also required airtight security, reliable uptime, analytics tracking, and full Google indexing. The goal was to deliver a high performance, maintainable, and secure full stack platform aligned with business growth.
items.Process:
1. Planning and Architecture
The project began with strategic planning to ensure scalability and modularity. A headless architecture was adopted, using Strapi for content management and Next.js for rendering. The priorities were performance, security, CMS independence, and strong CI/CD deployment.

2. Front End Development
Next.js was implemented with Static Site Generation and Incremental Static Regeneration. TailwindCSS provided responsive, consistent styling. Features included mobile first design, click to WhatsApp chat, optimized images, and accessibility support.

3. Backend and Content Management
Strapi CMS allowed recruiters to create, edit, and publish content without developer intervention. APIs connected Strapi to Next.js securely, with ISR ensuring new content appeared live instantly. The result was fast, dynamic content delivery with complete editorial control.

4. ATS and Job Board Integration
An Applicant Tracking System was added to allow job seekers to browse and apply for positions. Applications were securely stored and synchronized with backend systems, reducing manual HR tasks.

5. Security and Compliance
Security was prioritized across all layers:
- Content Security Policy configured through NGINX headers to prevent XSS and injection attacks by allowing only trusted content sources.
- HSTS enforced strict HTTPS only connections to block protocol downgrade.
- CORS restricted data access to trusted domains only.
- Cloudflare Turnstile protected the contact form.
- NGINX hardening with rate limiting, TLS 1.3, hidden headers, and automatic SSL renewal.
- Environment protection with secrets in env files and DO firewall rules.

6. Deployment and DevOps
The deployment pipeline was automated via GitHub Actions and hosted on DigitalOcean running Ubuntu Linux. Git ensured version control, while Bash scripts handled pulling updates, rebuilding, and restarting PM2 managed processes. NGINX acted as a reverse proxy and PM2 maintained uptime. This provided zero downtime, fast rollbacks, and continuous delivery.

7. SEO, Analytics, and Automation
SEO enhancements included structured schema, Open Graph tags, and automated sitemap submissions to Google Cloud Console. Google Analytics tracked user behavior for content strategy.

8. Performance and Scalability
SSG, ISR, and NGINX caching achieved sub two second load times. Cloudflare CDN reduced latency. PM2 clustering enabled multi core load balancing with 99.9 percent uptime.
items.Outcome: The new platform delivered a secure, high performance digital ecosystem that unified Gap Recruitment’s operations and online presence. The company gained a fully indexed, SEO optimized website, dynamic job board, and bot secure contact system. Security measures such as CSP, HSTS, and CORS ensured compliance and resilience. Automated deployments, PM2 monitoring, and NGINX optimization maintained consistent uptime and scalability.

items.title: Browns Pharmacy E-commerce on Zoho Commerce
items.teaser: Pharmacy store with 4,200+ SKUs, M Pesa checkout, and prescription controls. Automated imports, clean UI, and compliance ready shopping flows.
highlights: Problem: Move a large catalog online with local payments and rules.
Process: Scrape and cleanse data, bulk import to Zoho Inventory, conditional add to cart, M Pesa API.
Outcome: Three week launch, nationwide reach, automated catalog operations.
items.HeroSummary: A local pharmacy needed a robust online platform for 4,200 plus SKUs with prescription only logic and local payments. Using Zoho Commerce, custom scripts, and M Pesa integration, the store automated imports, enforced compliance rules, and reached customers nationwide within three weeks.
items.Problem: The pharmacy needed a modern e commerce site that could handle a large catalog, mark prescription only drugs, and support local payments through M Pesa. Manual management was not scalable.
items.Process: Built a custom scraper and data cleaning workflow, organized in Excel for bulk import into Zoho Inventory, then synced with Zoho Commerce. Implemented conditional add to cart for prescription items and integrated M Pesa. Customized styling for a compliant and clear UI.
items.Outcome: Within three weeks the pharmacy launched a fully functional e commerce platform. The pipeline reduced manual work by over 90 percent, enforced prescription rules, and enabled secure M Pesa transactions.

items.title: Mass Document Migration from Manatal to Zoho WorkDrive
items.teaser: Automated migration of 300,000 documents from Manatal to Zoho WorkDrive. Resilient pipeline with rate limit controls, retries, and zero data loss.
highlights: Problem: High vendor quote, need reliable bulk transfer.
Process: Node.js extract and load, cursor persistence, exponential backoff, PM2 on DO.
Outcome: 36 hours, 50 percent cost saving, verified integrity, reusable framework.
items.HeroSummary: A custom Node.js pipeline moved more than 300,000 documents from Manatal to Zoho WorkDrive at half the quoted cost. Deployed on DigitalOcean and managed with PM2, it handled API rate limits, checkpoints, and retries to complete a 36 hour migration with zero data loss and uninterrupted uptime.
items.Problem: The client needed to transfer over 300,000 documents from Manatal to Zoho WorkDrive but faced a 2000 USD quote that exceeded budget. They needed a reliable and affordable approach that guaranteed a complete and verified transfer without disruptions.
items.Process:
1. Requirements and discovery
Reviewed both platforms’ API docs to understand authentication, pagination, quotas, and rate limits. Mapped source fields to destination schemas and defined verification checks.

2. Architecture and design
Built a two stage extract and load pipeline with batching, checkpointing, idempotent writes, pagination cursors, exponential backoff, and structured logging.

3. Implementation and deployment
Wrote modular Node.js services, used streaming uploads, and deployed to a DigitalOcean droplet with PM2 for process supervision. Tuned concurrency to remain within limits and recover gracefully from transient failures.

4. Post transfer verification
Validated counts and spot checked content to confirm integrity and completeness.
items.Outcome: Migration completed in about 36 hours with zero data loss. Running the process on DigitalOcean ensured continuous execution. The client saved 50 percent versus the initial quote and retained a reusable framework for future migrations.
TechStack: Zoho WorkDrive REST and GraphQL APIs

items.title: InvoiceNow: Free Quotation and Invoice Generator
items.teaser: Free tool to create branded invoices and quotes in minutes. Logos, taxes, discounts, multi currency, preview and PDF download.
highlights: Problem: Slow and inconsistent Word templates.
Process: React UI, currency support, terms and notes, export to PDF, containerized deploy.
Outcome: Fast generation, consistent branding, zero cost to users.
items.HeroSummary: InvoiceNow is a free web tool for generating branded quotations and invoices with support for logos, taxes, discounts, and multiple currencies. Built with React and TypeScript, it offers live preview, reset, and PDF export, and is deployed in a portable, containerized setup.
items.Problem: Creating quotations and invoices in Word was slow and inconsistent. Users needed a fast and reliable way to produce professional documents with accurate totals and branding.
items.Process: Planned core features and UX, built a React and TypeScript front end, added multi currency, tax and discount options, notes and payment details, and enabled preview and PDF generation. Containerized with Docker and deployed on DigitalOcean with PM2. Analytics and sitemap submission were added for reach and improvement.
items.Outcome: Users can now create polished invoices and quotes in minutes at no cost. The platform ensures consistency and reduces manual work while supporting global use cases.

items.title: Gap Recruitment Visitor Management System
items.teaser: Self service check in and check out tied to SharePoint records. PWA kiosk with secure access and real time verification.
highlights: Problem: Manual logs and slow verification.
Process: Microsoft Graph lookup, controlled access PWA, timestamps, concurrency safe updates.
Outcome: Accurate records, instant feedback, analytics on punctuality trends.
items.HeroSummary: A self service visitor system validates interviewee details against SharePoint, records check in and check out, and provides instant feedback like early or late arrival notices. Built to run as a password protected PWA in kiosk like mode for on premise use.
items.Problem: Recruiters needed an automated way for interviewees to verify and log attendance without manual registers. The system had to work within Microsoft 365 and SharePoint with minimal friction and strong access control.
items.Process: Planned the SharePoint list structure and access model, built a simple web entry flow, verified details against uploaded records, and updated check in and check out timestamps. Added concurrency controls and polished user messages. Delivered as a password protected PWA usable only on premise.
items.Outcome: The system improved accuracy and reduced time spent on attendance. It also produced a reliable dataset for analytics, such as punctuality trends across weather and time slots.

items.title: Urbanac Cleaning Company One Page Website
items.teaser: Single page site that boosts trust and conversions for corporate clients. SEO, JSON LD, fast loads, and stable containerized hosting.
highlights: Problem: Weak online presence hurting bids.
Process: Responsive React build, structured metadata, Docker on DO, Nginx and PM2.
Outcome: Indexed fast site, higher inquiries, low maintenance footprint.
items.HeroSummary: Urbanac needed a credible online presence to win premium clients. I delivered a fast, responsive one page React site with strong SEO, JSON LD, and optimized assets, deployed on DigitalOcean with NGINX and PM2 for reliability.
items.Problem: Prospective clients hesitated to engage due to a poor online presence. Urbanac needed a single page website that looked professional, loaded quickly, and ranked well.
items.Process: Designed a minimal one page layout, built a responsive React front end, optimized images and assets, and implemented SEO metadata and JSON LD. Containerized with Docker, deployed to DigitalOcean, proxied via NGINX, and managed with PM2.
items.Outcome: Urbanac gained a trustworthy web presence with better search visibility and faster client inquiries. The DevOps stack ensures high uptime and simple maintenance.
